rationale.root_cause: It pinpoints the unsafe strcpy at program.c:10 copying argv[1] into char buf[256], and explains that an argument longer than 255 bytes overflows the stack buffer. It explicitly names this as a stack-buffer-overflow.
rationale.local_fix: It replaces the unbounded strcpy with a length-checked path that rejects overlong input (strlen(argv[1]) >= sizeof buf) and then copies with memcpy(strlen+1), preserving NUL-termination. This directly prevents overflow relative to the 256-byte destination.
rationale.global_fix: It recommends removing unbounded string handling by using a size-aware API that always carries capacity and centralizes validation, and banning strcpy/strcat. It also suggests dynamically sizing buffers (e.g., strdup) to eliminate fixed-size stack copies.
